name: salesforce-mcp-bridge
description: Query your Salesforce org from Claude (SOQL, SOSL, schema, recent records). Read-only. Works around the broken OAuth discovery on api.salesforce.com that blocks mcp-remote and Claude Connectors.
long_description: Stdio bridge to Salesforce Hosted MCP servers (sobject-reads, sobject-all). OAuth happens directly against your org's My Domain — no mcp-remote, no broken discovery metadata. Tokens are cached locally. Works with any Salesforce org — paste your org's My Domain and your External Client App Consumer Key in the fields below.

tools.name: getUserInfo
tools.description: Current user identity, role, and admin preferences.

tools.name: soqlQuery
tools.description: Execute a SOQL query against the org.

tools.name: listRecentSobjectRecords
tools.description: Records recently viewed or modified for a given object.

tools.name: getRelatedRecords
tools.description: Traverse relationships from a parent record (e.g. Contacts for an Account).

tools.name: getObjectSchema
tools.description: LLM-optimized schema for an object, or an index of all objects.

org_domain.title: Salesforce My Domain
org_domain.description: Your Salesforce org's My Domain, e.g. acme.my.salesforce.com (no https://, no trailing slash).

client_id.title: OAuth Consumer Key
client_id.description: Consumer Key from your Salesforce External Client App. Salesforce → Setup → External Client App Manager → your app → Settings → Consumer Key and Secret.

client_secret.title: OAuth Consumer Secret (leave empty for public client + PKCE)
client_secret.description: Only needed if your External Client App has 'Require secret for Web Server Flow' enabled. Salesforce's recommended MCP setup is public client + PKCE, so you can usually leave this empty.

server_name.title: MCP Server
server_name.description: Name of the Hosted MCP server to connect to. Default: platform/sobject-reads (read-only, 6 tools). For full CRUD, use platform/sobject-all.
